channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Harvey Weinstein can stay in hospital during #MeToo retrial, judge rules
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Harvey Weinstein has been moved to a New York City hospital after a judge approved the ailing ex-studio boss’s request to stay there rather than in jail when he’s not in court for his #MeToo retrial.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks CFPB layoffs, probes potential violation of order
item.description: A federal judge temporarily barred the Trump administration from laying off roughly 90 percent of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) as she mulls whether it violates her previous order. At a Friday hearing, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Worshippers return to restored Notre Dame for Easter rituals with emotion and awe
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Four months after its long-awaited reopening, Notre Dame Cathedral welcomed worshippers and tourists alike on Friday for an emotional Easter ceremony centered on the Crown of Thorns, a circular band of branches encased in a golden tube and one of Christianity’s most revered relics.
item.dc:creator: TOM NOUVIAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: FDA hiring contractors to replace terminated safety inspection staffers
item.description: When Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. announced sweeping job cuts at his department last month, he said that safety inspectors who oversee U.S. foods and drugs wouldn't be impacted.
item.dc:creator: By MATTHEW PERRONE AP Health Writer, Fox 5

item.title: FSU food service worker among those killed in shooting, company says
item.description: A food service worker was identified as one of two people killed when a gunman opened fire on the Florida State University campus Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KRON4

item.title: Military officials identify 2 Marines killed in crash
item.description: Two Camp Pendleton Marines killed in a crash during a convoy movement in New Mexico Tuesday have been identified.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pennsylvania senator asked child 'pornography' question during civics competition
item.description: "I am further appalled and furious that I now have to explain pornography to my fourth grader," the mom said.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, NewsNation

item.title: Military officials identify 2 Marines killed in crash in New Mexico
item.description: Two Camp Pendleton Marines killed in a crash during a convoy movement in New Mexico Tuesday have been identified.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, KREX 5

item.title: Wisconsin governor can lock in 400-year school funding increase using a veto, court says
item.description: The Wisconsin Supreme Court has ruled that the Democratic governor legally used the state’s uniquely powerful veto to lock in a school funding increase for 400 years.
item.dc:creator: Scott Bauer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Florida State gunman used deputy mom's former service weapon to kill 2 and wound 6, authorities say
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — The 20-year-old son of a sheriff’s deputy opened fire Thursday at Florida State University with his mother’s former service weapon, killing two men and wounding at least six others, investigators said.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE and DAVID FISCHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Military officials identify two Marines killed in crash in New Mexico
item.description: Two Camp Pendleton Marines killed in a crash during a convoy movement in New Mexico Tuesday have been identified.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, KTLA 5

item.title: Officials demand answers as crews work to restore power after another Puerto Rico blackout
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Crews scrambled to restore power to Puerto Rico on Thursday after a blackout hit the entire island the previous day, affecting the main international airport, hospitals and hotels filled with Easter vacationers.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FSU shooting: Location offers insight into gunman's motive
item.description: Authorities identified the suspect as 20-year-old student Phoenix Ikner, the son of a Leon County sheriff's deputy.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Train collides with funeral home limo in Tennessee; family of 6 injured
item.description: A funeral limo en route to a funeral — and carrying a family to the funeral — was struck by a train on Thursday in Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: David Royer, KRON4

item.title: Colorado man convicted of killing his wife after posing as her ex-boyfriend and stalking her
item.description: BROOMFIELD, Colo. (AP) — A Colorado man was convicted Thursday of killing his wife after stalking her while posing as her ex-boyfriend. Jurors reached the verdict against Daniel Krug, 44, a day after beginning deliberations.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Student who shot 4 at Dallas high school was targeting specific student, school police chief says
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — The student who shot four students when he opened fire at a Dallas high school this week was in and out of the building in less than two minutes and is believed to have been targeting a specific student, the school district police chief said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court to hear rare May arguments in birthright citizenship battle
item.description: The Supreme Court scheduled a rare May oral argument on the Trump administration’s emergency request to narrow a series of nationwide blocks on President Trump’s executive order that would restrict birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Wrong man, wrong bed, wrong treatment: Patient's death prompts medical board to discipline California doctor
item.description: A California physician has been placed on five years’ probation by the state's medical board for administering a course of treatment to a patient that was intended for the patient in an adjacent hospital bed.
item.dc:creator: Robert Price, Fox 5

item.title: Humpback whale deaths increasing on East Coast
item.description: A large number of humpback whales washing up on shores have been found to have trauma caused by humans.
item.dc:creator: Paxton Coley, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans consider increasing taxes on the rich in break from party orthodoxy
item.description: Republicans in Congress are considering increasing taxes on the rich as a part of President Trump’s “big, beautiful bill” of ambitious legislative priorities, a striking development that breaks with decades of party orthodoxy and is spurring alarm bells from traditional conservatives.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, ABC 4

item.title: Power blackout hits all of Puerto Rico as residents prepare for Easter weekend
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — A power blackout hit all of Puerto Rico on Wednesday as the heavily Catholic U.S. territory prepared to celebrate the Easter weekend.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 Americans kidnapped outside popular Juarez billiards
item.description: Two U.S. citizens have been rescued after being kidnapped outside a popular bar/restaurant in Juarez, Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Fernie Ortiz, KTLA 5

item.title: NY prison guards beat an inmate to death then tried to cover it up, prosecutors say
item.description: UTICA, N.Y. (AP) — Ten New York prison guards were charged Wednesday in connection with the fatal beating of a 22-year-old inmate last month — including two charged with murder. It's the second time a group of correctional officers in the state was indicted for a death behind bars this year.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL and DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: AmeriCorps members who respond to disasters and help nonprofits are let go in DOGE cuts
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Young volunteers who respond to natural disasters and help with community projects across the U.S. have been discharged as a result of the Trump administration 's campaign to shrink government workforce and services.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration issues order to stop construction on New York offshore wind project
item.description: The Trump administration issued an order Wednesday to stop construction on a major offshore wind project to power more than 500,000 New York homes, the latest in a series of moves targeting the industry.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: St. Pete woman reunites with nurse who saved her life after crash
item.description: A Pinellas County woman got the chance to reunite with the nurse who helped save her life after a crash two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Marino, WFLA, NBC

item.title: North Bay town named among 'best in the West' by USA Today
item.description: (KRON) -- One of the best small towns in the western part of the country is here in the Bay Area, according to USA Today's "10Best" rankings. Healdsburg, located in Sonoma County, was ranked as the third-best small town to visit.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Man in tactical gear attacks officers with pepper spray at Boston-area courthouse, video shows
item.description: Police have arrested a man who they say entered a courthouse while wearing a gas mask and tactical gear and attacked several people with pepper spray.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Utah Tech University holds early graduation ceremony for international students
item.description: On Tuesday, an early graduation celebration took place for some international students in southern Utah.
item.dc:creator: McKenzie Diaz, ABC 4

item.title: Suspect in shooting at Dallas high school is in custody, officials say
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — A suspect in a shooting at a Dallas high school that wounded four students and drew a heavy police response to the campus has been taken into custody, school district officials announced Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about Hollywood icon Gene Hackman's and his wife's deaths
item.description: Authorities in northern New Mexico released police body camera video and other public records Tuesday in the investigation into the deaths of Gene Hackman and his wife, Betsy Arakawa. The two were found dead Feb. 26 in their Santa Fe home.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Santa Fe sheriff releases body camera footage of Gene Hackman investigation
item.description: Newly-released body camera footage shows the confusion and questions investigators had after actor Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa were found dead in their home.
item.dc:creator: Marilyn Upchurch, WGN 9

item.title: US judge presses Trump administration on its refusal to return Kilmar Abrego Garcia
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. (AP) — A federal judge said Tuesday that she will order sworn testimony by Trump administration officials to determine if they complied with her orders to facilitate the return of Kilmar Abrego Garcia, who was mistakenly deported to a notorious El Salvador prison. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A look at the universities with federal funding targeted by the Trump administration
item.description: Harvard University is the latest in a growing list of higher education institutions that had its federal funding targeted by the government in order to comply with the Trump administration's political agenda. The series of threats — and subsequent pauses in funding — to some of the top U.S.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A Palestinian activist expecting a US citizenship interview is arrested instead by ICE in Vermont
item.description: A Palestinian man who led protests against the war in Gaza as a student at Columbia University was arrested Monday at a Vermont immigration office where he expected to be interviewed about finalizing his U.S. citizenship, his attorneys said.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, ABC 4
